frequentist probability , which claims that the probability of a random event denotes the relative frequency of occurrence
propensity probability , which interprets probability as the tendency of some experiment to yield a certain outcome
Bayesian probability , which includes expert knowledge as well as experimental data to produce probabilities
By Aumann's agreement theorem , Bayesian agents whose prior beliefs are similar will end up with similar posterior beliefs
